authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 438.66 - State monitoring requirements
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal regulation for Medicaid managed care program monitoring. It directly supports oversight evidence, reporting, enrollment, grievance, quality, and program-monitoring artifact boundaries relevant to program administration.

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 438.242 - Health information systems
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal regulation for managed care data systems, collection, analysis, integration, and reporting. It grounds truth-set and reporting-system expectations for rosters, encounter-related inputs, and program reports.

authoritative_sources.title: Quality of Care External Quality Review
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS Medicaid guidance describes annual EQR activities, technical reports, validated performance-measure data, posting, notification, and retention expectations. It is directly useful for recurring program reporting calendars and evidence packets.
used_for: validated measure and technical report artifact handling

authoritative_sources.title: Adult and Child Health Care Quality Measures
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS Medicaid quality measure guidance grounds Core Set reporting, measure collection, reporting period, and submission terminology used by healthcare programs and plans.

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Managed Care Manual, Chapter 2 - Medicare Advantage Enrollment and Disenrollment
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS manual for MA enrollment and disenrollment operations. It supports roster, member status, enrollment effective date, plan communication, and payer-owner escalation boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: roster, enrollment list, and member-status dependency handling

authoritative_sources.title: Summary of the HIPAA Privacy Rule
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the HIPAA privacy regulator. The summary is authoritative for role-based access, minimum necessary, designated record set, amendment, notice, and disclosure accounting concepts that constrain program records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PHI access, retention, and disclosure-control validation

authoritative_sources.title: Uses and Disclosures for Treatment, Payment, and Health Care Operations
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR guidance directly constrains PHI use and disclosure for treatment, payment, and health care operations, which is central when program rosters and reporting packets include patient or member data.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: privacy-sensitive handoff and program operation handling

authoritative_sources.title: United States Core Data for Interoperability Version 6
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy / Office of the National Coordinator for Health IT
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Federal interoperability baseline for healthcare data classes. It supports patient, encounter, coverage, care team, clinical note, and other terminology normalization when program records reference EHR or member-system fields.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: healthcare data terminology and artifact normalization

authoritative_sources.title: HEDIS Measures and Technical Resources
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NCQA is the recognized HEDIS steward for healthcare quality measurement. It grounds denominator, numerator, data source, audit, and submission terminology without authorizing this lane to interpret specifications.

authoritative_sources.title: Hospital Inpatient Quality Reporting Program
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS QualityNet program reference for provider quality reporting operations, data submission, measure resources, and calendars. It supports recurring reporting readiness without converting this lane into quality adjudication.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: provider reporting calendar and submission tracking

workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
workflow_questions.answer: Required prerequisites include tenant program type, business unit, jurisdiction, service setting, payer or plan, provider type, patient or member population, service dates, reporting period, roster inclusion rule, source-of-truth hierarchy, role-based access, privacy/minimum-necessary posture, retention schedule, owner map, required approvals, source artifacts, authorization or referral status when applicable, eligibility status, and submission deadline.

workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
workflow_questions.answer: Truth-set systems may include EHR or member systems, program management tools, CRM, case management, document management, reporting tracker systems, payer portals, eligibility systems, authorization/referral systems, quality reporting portals, privacy queues, and audit logs. Tenant source hierarchy determines which system controls a given field when systems conflict.

workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops before diagnosis, treatment planning, care-pathway changes, symptom triage, patient-safety judgment, medical necessity, coverage, claim adjudication, authorization approval, payer policy interpretation, privacy disclosure decisions, legal interpretation, compliance signoff, financial approval, and program policy approval. These require adjacent owner routing or orchestrator return.

workflow_stages.name: Program administration intake and boundary classification
workflow_stages.description: Capture request intent, program type, business unit, jurisdiction, payer or plan, service setting, patient/member population, reporting period, requested action, and authority context before treating the request as actionable.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Record and system-of-truth validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate rosters, enrollment lists, program charters, policy packets, status trackers, reporting calendars, case or CRM records, document repository artifacts, referral or authorization status, eligibility status, privacy posture, and audit trail requirements.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Submission and deadline readiness
workflow_stages.description: Check owner inputs, attachments, source files, reporting period, data-source mapping, measure or program terminology, deadline, prior approvals, retention classification, and authorized submission owner before declaring a packet ready.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Permitted administrative maintenance
workflow_stages.description: Update only permitted administrative fields such as owner, due date, tracker status, attachment receipt, evidence link, or routing status when source support, tenant permissions, and audit logging exist.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Retention, handoff, and publication handling
workflow_stages.description: Prepare status notes, evidence packets, submission-readiness checklists, and audit summaries under role-based access, PHI, retention, publication, and minimum-necessary controls.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Escalation for missing authority or conflicting records
workflow_stages.description: Escalate missing tenant facts, conflicting system records, missing authorization/referral/eligibility dependencies, PHI handling uncertainty, policy conflicts, clinical safety concerns, payer interpretation, compliance signoff, and program leadership approvals.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

allowed: Determine whether required administrative records, deadlines, attachments, owner inputs, and submissions are complete or blocked.
Route missing approvals, reporting inputs, participant data, authorization or referral dependencies, eligibility gaps, or privacy questions to the correct owner.
Maintain permitted administrative metadata only with source evidence, tenant permission, and audit logging.
Prepare program-administration status notes, submission-readiness checklists, evidence packets, and exception summaries.
escalate: Escalate when authorization, referral, eligibility, coverage, payer response, clinical documentation, privacy, owner, or deadline dependencies are missing or conflicting.
Escalate when public guidance, tenant policy, payer rule, contract, grant term, facility policy, or jurisdictional requirement materially conflicts.
Escalate when PHI handling, patient safety, clinical risk, payer interpretation, compliance signoff, legal interpretation, financial approval, or program policy approval is required.
refuse: Refuse to decide eligibility, coverage, authorization, medical necessity, claim, quality result, program approval, compliance attestation, legal outcome, financial approval, or clinical care.
Refuse unsupported official record alteration, backdating, deletion, concealment, or approval marking.
Refuse PHI disclosure, roster sharing, or broadened access without tenant privacy authority and minimum-necessary controls.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Patient or member harm from bad routing or missing escalation
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Escalate clinical risk, referral/authorization blockers, patient-safety signals, and unresolved owner conflicts instead of closing administrative tasks.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: PHI exposure or privacy violation from overbroad roster sharing
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Require role-based access, tenant privacy policy, consent context, minimum-necessary review, and privacy owner escalation for disclosure or external sharing.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Compliance exposure from unsupported submission readiness
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Require source links, owner inputs, attachments, reporting period, authority, and conflict-free source hierarchy before readiness is declared.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Revenue leakage or claim denial from missing authorization/referral dependency
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Track authorization, referral, eligibility, and documentation prerequisites as blockers and route to payer/utilization owners.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: False official record state from unsupported edits
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Refuse backdating, deletion, concealment, unsupported approvals, and official record manipulation; preserve audit trail.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
A required program submission is missing owner input or evidence
Administrative requests spill into policy approval or regulated judgment
Roster or participant data conflicts across systems

required_domain_scenario_family_mapping.required_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation

scenario_design_constraints: Normal execution must use healthcare program terminology and real artifact shapes, not generic project-management language.
Every scenario that touches PHI must preserve minimum-necessary and role-access handling.
Scenarios must prove refusal or escalation for clinical judgment, payer interpretation, privacy disclosure decisions, policy approval, unsupported record changes, and source conflicts.
Coverage, authorization, referral, eligibility, and documentation dependencies must be handled as prerequisites or blockers, not interpreted outcomes.

unresolved_ambiguity.topic: Tenant-specific program, payer, plan, and jurisdiction rules
unresolved_ambiguity.handling: Require tenant retrieval at runtime and escalate when missing or conflicting. Public baseline does not choose a tenant rule.

unresolved_ambiguity.topic: Whether a specific quality measure or payer rule permits a late attachment or roster correction
unresolved_ambiguity.handling: Route to quality, payer, compliance, or program leadership owner with source conflict preserved.

unresolved_ambiguity.topic: Exact retention window for a tenant's program reporting packets
unresolved_ambiguity.handling: Use tenant retention schedule and stricter privacy/retention rule when supplied; escalate if absent.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific program, payer, plan, and jurisdiction rules
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant retrieval at runtime and escalate when missing or conflicting. Public baseline does not choose a tenant rule.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Whether a specific quality measure or payer rule permits a late attachment or roster correction
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Route to quality, payer, compliance, or program leadership owner with source conflict preserved.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Exact retention window for a tenant's program reporting packets
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Use tenant retention schedule and stricter privacy/retention rule when supplied; escalate if absent.

market_ready_research_conclusion: The public source set covers regulator constraints, payer/provider operations, workflow and measure reporting, privacy handling, terminology, evidence expectations, and failure handling. No blocking ambiguity remains for a specification-based validated package because tenant-specific variants are explicitly modeled as runtime prerequisites and escalation conditions.
